pages.text:
Every morning, before breakfast, Leo opened his bedroom door.

And every morning…

Pip was already waiting.

Wearing his tiny yellow backpack.

Smiling.

"Ready?" asked Pip.

Leo grinned.

"I wonder what adventure we'll find today."

As they tiptoed down the hallway, Dad called from the kitchen.

"Morning, Leo! Shoes on please, we're leaving soon."

"Okay!" called Leo.

He really meant it.

Leo turned towards the front door.

Pip skipped beside him.

Halfway there…

Pip stopped.

His big eyes grew even bigger.

On the floor sat a tiny green Lego brick.

"Look!" whispered Pip.

Leo picked it up carefully.

"I've been looking for this!"

"The spaceship!" said Leo.

"It needs one more wing."

They hurried to Leo's room.

Leo clicked the green brick into place.

Pip stepped back.

"It still looks a little wobbly."

Leo nodded.

"It needs balancing."

"What if…" said Pip slowly.

"…spaceships had two extra wings?"

Leo's eyes lit up.

"They'd fly even better!"

Soon there were bricks everywhere.

Blue bricks. Grey bricks. Tiny transparent windows. Long white rockets.

The spaceship grew bigger and bigger.

Pip looked closely.

"Hmm…"

"What?"

"It doesn't have anywhere for the captain to sit."

Leo gasped.

"It needs seats!"

"And buttons!"

"And a snack cupboard!"

Just then…

"Meeooow."

Whiskers the cat squeezed into the cardboard spaceship box.

Leo laughed.

"The captain has arrived!"

Pip saluted.

"Captain Whiskers!"

The cat purred proudly.

"If Whiskers is flying to the Moon…" said Pip, "…what will he eat?"

Leo thought very hard.

"Moon fish?"

"Space biscuits?"

"Cheese?"

Both of them burst into giggles.

Dad appeared at the bedroom door.

He smiled when he saw the enormous spaceship.

"It looks amazing."

Leo beamed.

"We found the green brick!"

"And then Captain Whiskers…"

"And then…"

Leo stopped.

Dad smiled gently.

"Weren't you heading somewhere first?"

Leo blinked.

"I was?"

Leo looked around the room.

The spaceship. The cat. The bricks. The cardboard box.

Then he looked down.

He was still wearing his slippers.

"Oh!" he whispered.

"My shoes."

"I thought I'd only stopped for a tiny moment."

Dad nodded.

"I know."

Leo looked puzzled.

"It still feels like a tiny moment."

Pip looked puzzled too.

"It felt tiny to me as well."

Nobody was cross.

Everybody was thinking.

Dad picked up the little green brick.

He placed it gently into Pip's tiny backpack.

"Looks like today's adventure started here."

Leo smiled.

"It was a really good adventure."

Pip smiled too.

"It was."

Dad winked.

"And next time…"

Leo laughed.

"…maybe the shoes can come first."

Pip zipped up the backpack.

Another adventure. Another treasure. Another story to remember.
